name: Hermes HBC
summary: Hermes Bytecode — the executable form of Meta's Hermes JavaScript engine, used in React Native. Register-based and compact; what makes it distinctive is the shipping model — Hermes precompiles JS to HBC at app build time and ships only HBC to devices, not source. There is no JIT; HBC is interpreted.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: Hermes Bytecode is the executable form of Meta's Hermes JavaScript engine, used in React Native. HBC is register-based and compact like other modern bytecodes; what makes it distinctive is the *shipping model*. Hermes precompiles JS to HBC at app build time and ships only HBC to devices, not source — unusual among JS engines. There is no JIT; HBC is interpreted.

sections.title: Trade-offs and status
sections.content:
The trade-off: Hermes JS code runs 5–10× slower than V8 once warm, but starts 30–50% faster cold — the right trade for a mobile bundler.

Status (as of 2026-04): figures match the public Hermes design notes; Hermes remains JIT-less, while V8/JSC continue to add tiers, so the steady-state gap is unlikely to close. The ship-only-bytecode model (no source on device) also removes a class of reverse-engineering surface, useful for shipped mobile apps.
